name: FTX_WiFi_Red
summary: Reference guides for a Field Training Exercise (FTX) covering wireless network exploitation and GPS-based geolocation of a target access point.

documentation.readme: Reference guides for a Field Training Exercise (FTX) covering wireless network exploitation and GPS-based geolocation of a target access point.

arguments.name: -O
arguments.description: CLI flag '-O' accepted by the tool; behavior is implementation-defined and should be confirmed against the upstream README.

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --port
arguments.description: Target port (default: service default).

arguments.name: --interface
arguments.description: Network interface to bind to (e.g. wlan0mon, eth0).

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --verbose
arguments.description: Increase log verbosity (repeatable for higher levels).

arguments.name: --threads
arguments.description: Number of concurrent workers.

arguments.name: --timeout
arguments.description: Per-operation timeout in seconds.

arguments.name: --rate
arguments.description: Packet / request rate limit (packets/sec).

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --proxy
arguments.description: HTTP or SOCKS proxy URL for outbound requests.

arguments.name: --user-agent
arguments.description: HTTP User-Agent header value.

arguments.name: --cookie
arguments.description: HTTP Cookie header value.

arguments.name: --header
arguments.description: Extra HTTP header in key:value form (repeatable).

arguments.name: --insecure
arguments.description: Skip TLS certificate verification (lab use only).

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Emit JSON output instead of human-readable text.

arguments.name: --no-color
arguments.description: Disable ANSI color in output.

arguments.name: --quiet
arguments.description: Suppress non-error output.

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --yes
arguments.description: Skip confirmation prompts (use with care).

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

arguments.name: --no-banner
arguments.description: Suppress banner / tool-name output.

arguments.name: --capture
arguments.description: Capture mode: write packets to a pcap file.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with yuloli/deauth (score=9); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with x90er/krackattacks-scripts (score=9); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with wranders/wireless-probe-analyzer (score=9); pair in the chain planner.

Phase 3: same attack_surface=['wifi_2_4_ghz', 'wifi_5_ghz', 'shell_linux'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [wifi_2_4_ghz, wifi_5_ghz, shell_linux] in the Wireless category. Fits when the engagement needs: Reference guides for a Field Training Exercise (FTX) covering wireless network exploitation and GPS-based geolocation of a target access point.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: Reference guides for a Field Training Exercise (FTX) covering wireless network exploitation and GPS-based geolocation of a target access point.
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `sudo gunzip /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt.gz`. Further invocations from upstream: `wget -q -O /tmp/google-earth.deb $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL`; `sudo dpkg -i /tmp/google-earth.deb`. Reference guides for a Field Training Exercise (FTX) covering wireless network exploitation and GPS-based geolocation of a target access point.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: When you need Wireless, use FTX_WiFi_Red because Reference guides for a Field Training Exercise (FTX) covering wireless network exploitation and GPS-based geolocation of a target access point.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `sudo gunzip /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt.gz` as documented upstream for FTX_WiFi_Red.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `wget -q -O /tmp/google-earth.deb $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL` as documented upstream for FTX_WiFi_Red.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `sudo dpkg -i /tmp/google-earth.deb` as documented upstream for FTX_WiFi_Red.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `sudo apt-get install -f -y   # resolve any dependency issues` as documented upstream for FTX_WiFi_Red.
When chaining Wireless tools, pair FTX_WiFi_Red with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `FTX_WiFi_Red --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
command_examples: sudo apt-get install -f -y   # resolve any dependency issues
